entries.definition: a change of route or direction, especially an official detour for traffic
entries.exampleSentence: There's a diversion in place due to roadworks on the bridge — follow the yellow signs.

entries.definition: something that takes attention away from something else; a distraction or tactic to divert focus
entries.exampleSentence: The press release was just a diversion to draw attention away from the scandal.

entries.definition: an enjoyable activity that amuses or entertains; a pastime
entries.exampleSentence: For him, reading detective novels was a pleasant diversion from the stresses of work.
